picks.rationale: The Philadelphia Phillies hold a significant pitching advantage with Cristopher Sanchez (9-3, 2.13 ERA), who ranks fourth in MLB and boasts an elite 0.95 ERA at home. He faces Pittsburgh's Bubba Chandler (3-7, 4.42 ERA), who has shown inconsistency and command issues. The Phillies have a strong 23-20 home record and a favorable head-to-head record against the Pirates in Philadelphia, winning three of their last four matchups. This pitching mismatch and home advantage provide a strong edge for the Phillies to cover the run line.

This game features a premier pitching matchup between the Rangers' Jacob deGrom (6-5, 3.55 ERA) and the Guardians' Tanner Bibee (2-8, 3.78 ERA), both of whom have strong historical performances against their respective opponents. DeGrom has been particularly sharp in June with a 1.69 ERA over his last three starts, and Bibee has allowed two earned runs or fewer in three of his last four outings. Furthermore, both teams are missing key offensive players, including José Ramírez for Cleveland and Wyatt Langford and Danny Jansen for Texas, which points to a low-scoring affair. The line movement on the total also indicates sharp money on the under.

The Milwaukee Brewers have dominated the Cincinnati Reds this season, holding a perfect 4-0 head-to-head record and winning the series opener 5-3. Milwaukee boasts a strong 51-31 overall record and an impressive 27-17 mark at home, while the Reds have a poor 19-49 record in their last 68 games against the Brewers. Brewers' starter Brandon Sproat recently shut down the Reds with six scoreless innings and 10 strikeouts, giving Milwaukee a significant pitching advantage. The Brewers have also covered the run line in six consecutive Tuesday games against National League opponents.

The Los Angeles Dodgers are in excellent form, having won three consecutive games and six of their last seven on this road trip, including a 9-4 victory yesterday against the Athletics. They face an Athletics team that has lost three straight and seven of their last nine games. The pitching matchup heavily favors the Dodgers with Justin Wrobleski (9-2, 2.71 ERA) against Jeffrey Springs (3-7, 5.52 ERA), whose underlying metrics suggest significant regression. Additionally, the Athletics' bullpen has been a disaster at home with an ERA above six, while the Dodgers' bullpen has been reliable on the road, further solidifying the Dodgers' advantage.

The Washington Nationals offer strong value as road underdogs, despite having a better overall record (43-43) than the Boston Red Sox (37-46). Washington boasts an impressive 26-18 road record this season and has won nine of their last ten games as road underdogs following a road loss. Nationals' starter Cade Cavalli has been consistently effective, holding opponents to three earned runs or fewer in eight straight starts. Conversely, the Red Sox have struggled at home, losing 12 of their last 17 games as favorites after a home win, making the Nationals a high-confidence pick at plus money.

Norway's key attacking players, Erling Haaland and Martin Odegaard, return to the starting lineup after being strategically rested, significantly boosting their offensive threat. Both teams have shown a propensity for higher-scoring games, with Norway's World Cup matches averaging five goals and Ivory Coast's recent matches frequently exceeding 2.5 goals. The verified line of Over 1.5 at -220 reflects this expectation, and with the knockout stage intensity, both teams will be pushing for goals. Ivory Coast also has a doubtful player in Wilfried Singo (hamstring), which could further impact their defense.

France has been in prolific scoring form, netting two or more goals in 15 of their last 16 matches. Sweden's recent form also points to high-scoring affairs, with seven of their last nine international games seeing over 3.5 goals and both teams scoring in their last nine. The head-to-head history further supports this, with 75% of their last four meetings exceeding 2.5 goals. With Sweden missing defender Isak Hien due to a hamstring injury, France's attack should find opportunities to contribute to a high-scoring contest.

Mexico has been defensively stout, keeping five clean sheets in their last six matches and conceding only one goal during that span. Ecuador also boasts a strong defensive record, having conceded only five goals in 18 qualifiers and not allowing two or more goals in their last 26 games. Furthermore, two of the last three head-to-head encounters between these teams have ended in goalless draws, indicating a history of tight, low-scoring affairs. The high odds for the Under 1.5 at +135 present significant value given these defensive trends and the knockout stage pressure.

Norway sees the return of star players Erling Haaland and Martin Odegaard to the starting lineup after they were strategically rested, providing a significant boost to their attacking prowess. The Opta supercomputer also projects Norway as the clear favorites to advance in this Round of 32 clash. While Ivory Coast has a slight edge in the overall head-to-head record, this is their first competitive meeting, and Norway's full-strength squad is expected to dominate. Ivory Coast also has a doubtful player in Wilfried Singo (hamstring), which could further weaken their defense against a potent Norwegian attack.

France enters this Round of 32 match with impeccable form, having won all three of their group stage games and scoring 2+ goals in 15 of their last 16 matches. Their head-to-head record against Sweden is dominant, with France winning 12 of 23 meetings and four of their last five encounters. Sweden, on the other hand, is missing key defender Isak Hien due to injury, which will further challenge their ability to contain France's potent offense led by Kylian Mbappe. Despite some minor injury concerns for France, William Saliba is expected to return to bolster their defense.
